title: docs: buildah-from documents --security-opt no-new-privileges as not supported
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,
I was testing all buildah cli commands to understand how it works and realized that the documentation is not matching the code.

See: 
https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/6b35d41d04c09381cc30520d77dc7287bb22cf87/docs/buildah-from.1.md?plain=1#L425

https://github.com/podman-container-tools/buildah/blob/6b35d41d04c09381cc30520d77dc7287bb22cf87/pkg/parse/parse.go#L311-L316


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Create the working container
``` bash
./bin/buildah --root /tmp/buildah-test-d78xh1ch/root --runroot /tmp/buildah-test-d78xh1ch/runroot --network none from --security-opt no-new-privileges docker.io/library/alpine
```
2. Check if no new privilges is set
``` bash
./bin/buildah --root /tmp/buildah-test-d78xh1ch/root --runroot /tmp/buildah-test-d78xh1ch/runroot run alpine-working-container -- grep NoNewPrivs /proc/self/status
```


### Describe the results you received

3. Expected output:
```bash
NoNewPrivs:     1
```

Buildah.json sets the value `"NoNewPrivileges":true,`.

### Describe the results you expected

3. Expected output:
```bash
NoNewPrivs:     0
```

Buildah.json sets the value `"NoNewPrivileges":false,`.

### buildah version output

```yaml
lm00@lm00:~/src/buildah$ ./bin/buildah version
Version:         1.45.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.25.11 X:nodwarf5
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.3.0
image Version:   5.41.0-dev
Git Commit:      51c40cfc557e129248bb477510d18f5ca69a87dd
Built:           Thu Jul  9 21:41:40 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
-
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Oof, looks like this was overlooked in #4402.  Thanks for #6952!
